name: Chocolate-Caramel-Pecan Cheesecake
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine graham cracker crumbs and butter in a bowl. Stir until well mixed.
2. Press the mixture evenly onto the bottom and 1 inch up the sides of a 9-inch springform pan.
3. Unwrap the caramels and combine with evaporated milk in a saucepan. Heat over low heat, stirring often until the caramels are melted.
4. Pour the caramel mixture over the graham cracker crust.
5. Sprinkle chopped pecans evenly over the caramel layer.
6. In another bowl, beat cream cheese at high speed until light and fluffy.
7. Gradually add sugar, mixing well after each addition.
8. Add eggs one at a time, mixing thoroughly after each egg is incorporated.
9. Stir in vanilla extract and chocolate morsels, beating until well blended.
10. Spoon the mixture over the pecan layer.
11. Bake at 350 degrees Fahrenheit for 30 minutes.
12. Turn off the oven heat and run a knife around the edge of the pan to release the sides.
13. Let cool to room temperature on a wire rack.
14. Cover and chill in the refrigerator for at least 8 hours before serving.
15. Top with pecan halves and serve.
